note: Right-sized intake workshop for a 1-2 SP repo-hygiene slice (Proposal 169) whose implementation was adopted ahead of governance. One light lens carries the only load-bearing design decision (the scorer classification); the eight skips were rendered with rationale in the agenda and confirmed by the maintainer.

agenda: ratify the classification: process-quality scorer is test infrastructure (tests/support/), not product runtime or a public evaluation harness
where does the generated report output land now that evaluation/report.md is deleted
is tests/support/ the right long-term home for shared test infrastructure
should any stub/pointer remain for a future public evaluation surface, or is the clean break correct
architecture-core.decision: Human confirmed: the scorer is test infrastructure with its supported surface being the two CI integration-test entry points; tests/support/ is the long-term home; generated report output moves to untracked scratch/test-result space; clean break (no stub) with only retirement-explanation wording in docs; integration-test entry points and CI job names stay frozen; any future public evaluation surface is a fresh governed slice.
architecture-core.diagram: workshop/architecture-core.md — before/after structure diagram (evaluation/ public surface -> tests/support/ test infrastructure), rendered in-band and confirmed

skipped.component-design: no new components; one script moves location ~unchanged (99% rename)
skipped.requirements-nfr: AC1-AC6 already enumerated in Proposal 169; the only NFR is that the tests still pass
skipped.ui-ux: no user interface; doc wording only
skipped.data-storage: no persisted data; report-output location covered by the architecture-core decision
skipped.security-compliance: no auth/secrets/privacy surface; deletions, a move, and doc edits
skipped.integration-api: the scorer's public surface is the two integration tests, explicitly preserved (AC2/AC3)
skipped.devops-operations: CI job names + test semantics explicitly out of scope; AC4 pins the Linux-safe path assertion
skipped.observability-resilience: no runtime behavior to observe or recover
